<!--
  Mimic PR template. Keep PRs small and single-concern. If this PR is growing
  past ~400 lines of meaningful change, stop and split it.

  Conventional commit prefixes: feat: / fix: / chore: / docs: / refactor: / test:
-->

## What changed

<!-- Short, factual description of the change. One concern per PR. -->

## Why

<!-- Motivation. Link to the issue, roadmap phase, or prior PR if relevant. -->

## How to test

<!--
  Concrete steps a reviewer (human or AI) can follow to verify the change.
  Include commands, URLs, or UI paths as appropriate.

  If this PR touches cadence playback or style output, include a manual demo
  step here — automated review cannot verify "does this feel right".
-->

## Risks / limitations

<!--
  Known unknowns, follow-ups, regressions to watch, anything intentionally
  out of scope. If there are no risks beyond the obvious, say so.
-->

## Checklist

- [ ] Branch is named `feat/...`, `fix/...`, `chore/...`, or `docs/...`
- [ ] Commit messages follow conventional commits
- [ ] Diff is small and single-concern (≲400 lines of meaningful change)
- [ ] CI is green (lint, type-check, tests where applicable)
- [ ] If this touches cadence playback or style output, a manual demo step is described above
